highlights.text: My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
I wrote yesterday: “In an “ultra bull case” ES won’t even retest 5652.
As readers know, we can tell when institutions are accumulating because all rallies in ES are caused by my core setup: The Failed Breakdown, which is a footprint of institutional accumulation.

Instead of just buying this, a safer bet is to wait for the Failed Breakdown of today’s low which is just a little above at 5607.” We saw exactly this Wednesday, we sold down to 5597-5600, put in a Failed Breakdown of Tuesdays lows at 5607-05 in the process, then rallied up the range.

Its been an incredible run and in today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into three high quality Failed Breakdowns we had Wednesday, including the Wednesday 5607 Failed Breakdown that triggered yesterdays 130+ point rally.
I’ll also talk the difference between a Failed Breakdown and a Level Reclaim.

post_title: Are Bulls Finally Running Out Of Steam? May 12th Plan
